arts.ttl: The Marc Guehi armband saga speaks volumes about the state of homophobia in football
arts.sum: Only 2 captains out of the 20 clubs made atypical stances on the Rainbow Laces campaign: Palace’s Marc Guehi and Ipswich Town’s Sam Morsy. As part of Rainbow Laces weekend, the Premier League’s campaign to promote equality and LGBTQ+ acceptance in England’s top flight, the captains of each team were given a different armband to the typical black and white - a rainbow one.

arts.ttl: Man City transfer news: Long-standing servant linked with 'sensational' return to former club
arts.sum: Manchester City defender Kyle Walker is reportedly keen on a return to Sheffield United, 15 years after departing clubs, over lucrative moves to Saudi Arabia or MLS.

arts.ttl: Papers: Beckham and Inter Miami target Man City star De Bruyne
arts.sum: The top stories and transfer rumours from Friday's newspapers...

arts.ttl: Crystal Palace to sign foreign wonderkid whose dad also played in England
arts.sum: Crystal Palace are closing in on the signing of Australian teenager Rylan Brownlie, according to reports. The 17-year-old is a product of A-League side Brisbane Roar and played 12 times for the club at senior level, notching one goal and one assist. Arsenal & Liverpool dealt major blow in pursuit of Crystal Palace wonderkid! arts.ttl: 'When De Bruyne starts, Man City win' - here's why
arts.sum: Match of the Day's Micah Richards and Martin Keown explain how Kevin de Bruyne inspired Man City to victory over Nottingham Forest, ending the club's seven-game winless streak.

arts.ttl: Rodri: 'I would take Nico Williams to Man City without a doubt'
arts.sum: Rodri has admitted he would ‘take’ Nico Williams to Manchester City ‘without a doubt’, with speculation surrounding the Athletic Bilbao winger still raging. The forward has continued to be a star man for Athletic as they push for Champions League qualification but reports have previously claimed that this will be Williams’ last season at the club.

arts.ttl: 2025 Club World Cup Draw RESULTS: Man City share a group with European heayweights, while Chelsea face a veteran Blues legend at tournament in the US next summer
arts.sum: Manchester City have been handed the headline match of the FIFA Club World Cup group stage as Pep Guardiola's side discovered their opponents for next summer's tournament.

arts.ttl: I sat through chaotic Club World Cup draw with Donald Trump surprise - Man City's reality is clear
arts.sum: Manchester City belatedly learned their FIFA Club World Cup opponents in a strange draw in Miami featuring an eccentric Giani Infantino and a cameo from Donald Trump.

arts.ttl: Rodri tells Man City to use their millions to sign £48m talent wanted by Arsenal this summer
arts.sum: Athletic Club’s Nico Williams is one of the most sought-after players in European football at present following his exceptional 2023/24 campaign for both club and country. The winger played a key role in Spain winning Euro 2024 in Germany this summer, which followed the 22-year-old’s displays that helped Athletic Club win the Copa del Rey […] The post Rodri tells Man City to use their millions to sign £48m talent wanted by Arsenal this summer appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Messi’s Inter Miami to open 2025 Club World Cup as Man City drawn with Juventus
arts.sum: Draw for revamped competition held in Miami Kylian Mbappé and Neymar to meet in group stage Lionel Messi and Inter Miami will open this year’s Club World Cup against Egyptian club Al Ahly, as Kylian Mbappé and Neymar face off for Real Madrid and Al-Hilal. Premier League champions Manchester City will face Juventus, Moroccan side Wydad AC and Al Ain of the United Arab Emirates in the opening group stage of Fifa’s controversial tournament, while Chelsea will take on Brazil giants Flamengo, Mexico’s Club Leon and Esperance Tunis.
